Question:
Using NHANES data from 2011-2016, we will do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights.
We will include participants whose age at screening is 20 years or older.
We will also exclude participants with incomplete any of the following PHQ-9 quesitons:  "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead".
We will also exclude participants who were pregnant at the time of the exam and exclude participants with incomplete any of the following demographic data: age at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Additionally, we will exclude participants with incomplete all of following diabetes exam or survey data: self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals, fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL), glycosylated hemoglobin (HbA1c) (%), post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl), whether they take glucose lowering agents, whether they take insulin.
Covariates will include age at screening categorized to 20-39, 40-59, 60-79, equal or more than 80, gender, race/Hispanic origin categorized into White, Black, Mexican Hispanic, Other Hispanic, Asian, others.
Our predictor is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), and all were added and categorized as None [0-4], Mild [5-9], Moderate [10-14], Moderately Severe [15-19], and Severe [20 or higher]): 
The outcome will be diabetes presence where participants who met one of the following were considered to have diabetes and else as no: fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL) ≥126 mg/dL, glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) %  ≥6.5%, post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl) ≥200 mg/dL, self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionalstaking glucose-lowering agents, taking insulin.
Structured Question:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2016

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20 years

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing any of the following PHQ-9 questions:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
- pregnant at the time of the exam
- missing any of the following demographic data:
    - age at screening
    - gender
    - race/Hispanic origin
- missing all of the following diabetes exam or survey data:
    - self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals
    - fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL)
    - glycosylated hemoglobin (HbA1c) (%)
    - post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl)
    - whether they take glucose lowering agents
    - whether they take insulin

Covariates:
- age at screening categorized as:
    - 20-39
    - 40-59
    - 60-79
    - >= 80
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    - White
    - Black
    - Mexican Hispanic
    - Other Hispanic
    - Asian
    - others

Predictor:
- depression outcome calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions, then categorized:
    - have little interest in doing things
    - feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
    - trouble sleeping or sleeping too much
    - feeling tired or having little energy
    - poor appetite or overeating
    - feeling bad about yourself
    - trouble concentrating on things
    - moving or speaking slowly or too fast
    - thought you would be better off dead
  Each question scored as:
    - Not at all (0)
    - Several days (1)
    - More than half the days (2)
    - Nearly every day (3)
  Categorized as:
    - None: [0-4]
    - Mild: [5-9]
    - Moderate: [10-14]
    - Moderately Severe: [15-19]
    - Severe: [20 or higher]

Outcome:
- diabetes presence = 1 if any of the following criteria are met; 0 otherwise:
    - fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL) >= 126 mg/dL
    - glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) % >= 6.5%
    - post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl) >= 200 mg/dL
    - self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals
    - taking glucose-lowering agents
    - taking insulin

-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_g
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_h
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_i
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL;


-- STEP 2: Create temp_psu_stratum

-- TEMP TABLE FOR PSU and Stratum
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_psu_stratum;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_psu_stratum AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_i;


-- STEP 3: Create final weight+design table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS weight_design;
CREATE TABLE weight_design AS
SELECT 
    s.respondent_sequence_number,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_stratum,
    w.survey_weight
FROM temp_psu_stratum s
INNER JOIN temp_master_weight w
  ON s.respondent_sequence_number = w.respondent_sequence_number;




-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age at screening (years)", "age at screening (years)", "age at screening (years)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening ON temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening (respondent_sequence_number);

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t1."have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    t2."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    t3."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    t4."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    t5."poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    t6."feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    t7."trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    t8."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    t9."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead",
    t10."pregnancy_status_at_exam",
    t11."gender",
    t12."race_hispanic_origin",
    t13."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t14."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
    t15."glycohemoglobin",
    t16."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl",
    t17."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar",
    t18."taking_insulin_now"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_have_little_interest_in_doing_things t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_poor_appetite_or_overeating t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_bad_about_yourself t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_trouble_concentrating_on_things t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead t9 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t9.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_pregnancy_status_at_exam t10 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t10.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t11 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t11.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t12 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t12.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t13 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t13.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl t14 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t14.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t15 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t15.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl t16 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t16.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar t17 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t17.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_taking_insulin_now t18 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t18.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);


-- Clean up final_master_table
DELETE FROM final_master_table
WHERE respondent_sequence_number NOT IN (
    SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM weight_design
);


-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all participants whose age at screening is 20 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with incomplete any of the PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who were pregnant at the time of the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with incomplete any of the demographic data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with incomplete all of the diabetes exam or survey data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
       f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IS NULL
   AND f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NULL
   AND f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL
   AND f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" IS NULL
   AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NULL
   AND f."taking_insulin_now" IS NULL
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age at screening categorized
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening_categorized AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 40 THEN '20-39'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 40 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '40-59'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 80 THEN '60-79'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 80 THEN '>= 80'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_at_screening_categorized"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin categorized
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin_categorized AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican hispanic'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other hispanic'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'others'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin_categorized"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Depression outcome
-- Step 1: Map each of the 9 PHQ-9 question answers to a score (0-3)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_interest",
    CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_depressed",
    CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_sleep",
    CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_energy",
    CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_appetite",
    CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_self_bad",
    CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_concentrating",
    CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_movement",
    CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (COALESCE("score_interest", 0) + COALESCE("score_depressed", 0) + COALESCE("score_sleep", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_energy", 0) + COALESCE("score_appetite", 0) + COALESCE("score_self_bad", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_concentrating", 0) + COALESCE("score_movement", 0) + COALESCE("score_dead", 0)) AS "depression_score_total"
FROM temp_phq9_scores;

-- Step 3: Bin depression score into custom categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 0 AND "depression_score_total" <= 4 THEN 'none'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 5 AND "depression_score_total" <= 9 THEN 'mild'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 10 AND "depression_score_total" <= 14 THEN 'moderate'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 15 AND "depression_score_total" <= 19 THEN 'moderately severe'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 20 THEN 'severe'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_score;

-- 4.5 Diabetes presence
-- Step 1: Determine if any diabetes criteria are met
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_criteria_met AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126.0 OR
     f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5 OR
     f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" >= 200.0 OR
     f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR
     f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' OR
     f."taking_insulin_now" = 'Yes') AS "has_diabetes_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Map to '1' or '0'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_presence AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "has_diabetes_raw" IS TRUE THEN '1'
        WHEN "has_diabetes_raw" IS FALSE THEN '0'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "diabetes_presence"
FROM temp_diabetes_criteria_met;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  ascateg."age_at_screening_categorized",
  g."gender",
  rhc."race_hispanic_origin_categorized",
  tdo."depression_outcome",
  dp."diabetes_presence"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_at_screening_categorized ascateg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ascateg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin_categorized rhc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rhc."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetes_presence dp ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dp."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- STEP: Create merged final table with design variables + in_analysis flag
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_final_with_weights;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_with_weights AS
SELECT 
    wd."respondent_sequence_number",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_psu",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_stratum",
    wd."survey_weight",
    CASE WHEN ft."respondent_sequence_number" IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS "in_analysis",
    ft."age_at_screening_categorized",
    ft."gender",
    ft."race_hispanic_origin_categorized",
    ft."depression_outcome",
    ft."diabetes_presence"
FROM weight_design wd
LEFT JOIN temp_final_table ft
  ON wd."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number";
  
-- Preview the result

        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

        category <- "weight"

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors

        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",

    # Convert binary/categorical variables to factor if not already
    for (var in factor_candidates) {

        df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
    }

    # Initialize reference_levels table
    reference_levels <- tibble(variable = character(), reference_level = character())

    for (col in factor_candidates) {

    idx <- is.na(y) & !is.na(x_chr)
    if (any(idx)) {

        ifelse(length(u) == 0, "<no non-missing values>", paste(u, collapse = ", "))

    formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + ")))
            
    
    survey_design <- svydesign(
        ids     = as.formula(paste0("~", psu_var)),
        strata  = as.formula(paste0("~", stratum_var)),
        weights = as.formula(paste0("~", weight_var)),
        data    = df,
        nest    = TRUE
        )
    survey_design <- subset(survey_design, in_analysis ==1)
        
    
    model <- svyglm(formula,design = survey_design, family = quasibinomial()) 
            
    
    result_table <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
            select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)

[''''

2. In the `CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table` statement, the alias `do` is used for the `temp_depression_outcome` table.

-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all participants whose age at screening is 20 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with incomplete any of the PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who were pregnant at the time of the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" NOT IN ('Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam');

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with incomplete any of the demographic data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with incomplete all of the diabetes exam or survey data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
       f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IS NULL
   AND f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NULL
   AND f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL
   AND f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" IS NULL
   AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NULL
   AND f."taking_insulin_now" IS NULL
);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age at screening categorized
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening_categorized AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 40 THEN '20-39'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 40 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 60 THEN '40-59'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" < 80 THEN '60-79'
           WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 80 THEN '>= 80'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "age_at_screening_categorized"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin categorized
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin_categorized AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican hispanic'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other hispanic'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'others'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin_categorized"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Depression outcome
-- Step 1: Map each of the 9 PHQ-9 question answers to a score (0-3)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_scores AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_interest",
    CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_depressed",
    CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_sleep",
    CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_energy",
    CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_appetite",
    CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_self_bad",
    CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_concentrating",
    CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_movement",
    CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
        WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
        WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
        WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
        WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "score_dead"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Calculate total depression score
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_score AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    (COALESCE("score_interest", 0) + COALESCE("score_depressed", 0) + COALESCE("score_sleep", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_energy", 0) + COALESCE("score_appetite", 0) + COALESCE("score_self_bad", 0) +
     COALESCE("score_concentrating", 0) + COALESCE("score_movement", 0) + COALESCE("score_dead", 0)) AS "depression_score_total"
FROM temp_phq9_scores;

-- Step 3: Bin depression score into custom categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 0 AND "depression_score_total" <= 4 THEN 'none'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 5 AND "depression_score_total" <= 9 THEN 'mild'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 10 AND "depression_score_total" <= 14 THEN 'moderate'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 15 AND "depression_score_total" <= 19 THEN 'moderately severe'
        WHEN "depression_score_total" >= 20 THEN 'severe'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_score;

-- 4.5 Diabetes presence
-- Step 1: Determine if any diabetes criteria are met
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_criteria_met AS
SELECT
    c."respondent_sequence_number",
    (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126.0 OR
     f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5 OR
     f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" >= 200.0 OR
     f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes' OR
     f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes' OR
     f."taking_insulin_now" = 'Yes') AS "has_diabetes_raw"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Map to '1' or '0'
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_presence AS
SELECT
    "respondent_sequence_number",
    CASE
        WHEN "has_diabetes_raw" IS TRUE THEN '1'
        WHEN "has_diabetes_raw" IS FALSE THEN '0'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "diabetes_presence"
FROM temp_diabetes_criteria_met;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  ascateg."age_at_screening_categorized",
  g."gender",
  rhc."race_hispanic_origin_categorized",
  tdo."depression_outcome",
  dp."diabetes_presence"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_at_screening_categorized ascateg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = ascateg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin_categorized rhc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rhc."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetes_presence dp ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dp."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;
```']

Question:
Using NHANES data from 2011-2016, we will do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights.
We will include participants whose age at screening is 20 years or older.
We will also exclude participants with incomplete any of the following PHQ-9 quesitons:  "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead".
We will also exclude participants who were pregnant at the time of the exam and exclude participants with incomplete any of the following demographic data: age at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Additionally, we will exclude participants with incomplete all of following diabetes exam or survey data: self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals, fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL), glycosylated hemoglobin (HbA1c) (%), post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl), whether they take glucose lowering agents, whether they take insulin.
Covariates will include age at screening categorized to 20-39, 40-59, 60-79, equal or more than 80, gender, race/Hispanic origin categorized into White, Black, Mexican Hispanic, Other Hispanic, Asian, others.
Our predictor is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), and all were added and categorized as None [0-4], Mild [5-9], Moderate [10-14], Moderately Severe [15-19], and Severe [20 or higher]): 
The outcome will be diabetes presence where participants who met one of the following were considered to have diabetes and else as no: fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL) ≥126 mg/dL, glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) %  ≥6.5%, post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl) ≥200 mg/dL, self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionalstaking glucose-lowering agents, taking insulin.

-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_g
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_h
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_i
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL;


-- STEP 2: Create temp_psu_stratum

-- TEMP TABLE FOR PSU and Stratum
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_psu_stratum;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_psu_stratum AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL

       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL

       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_i;


-- STEP 3: Create final weight+design table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS weight_design;
CREATE TABLE weight_design AS
SELECT 
    s.respondent_sequence_number,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_stratum,
    w.survey_weight
FROM temp_psu_stratum s
INNER JOIN temp_master_weight w
  ON s.respondent_sequence_number = w.respondent_sequence_number;

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t1."have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    t2."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    t3."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    t4."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    t5."poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    t6."feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    t7."trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    t8."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    t9."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead",
    t10."pregnancy_status_at_exam",
    t11."gender",
    t12."race_hispanic_origin",
    t13."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t14."fasting_glucose_mg_dl",
    t15."glycohemoglobin",
    t16."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl",
    t17."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar",
    t18."taking_insulin_now"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_have_little_interest_in_doing_things t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_poor_appetite_or_overeating t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_bad_about_yourself t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_trouble_concentrating_on_things t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead t9 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t9.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_pregnancy_status_at_exam t10 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t10.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t11 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t11.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t12 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t12.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t13 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t13.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_fasting_glucose_mg_dl t14 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t14.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t15 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t15.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl t16 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t16.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar t17 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t17.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_taking_insulin_now t18 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t18.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);


-- Clean up final_master_table
DELETE FROM final_master_table
WHERE respondent_sequence_number NOT IN (
    SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM weight_design
);


-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all participants whose age at screening is 20 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with incomplete any of the PHQ-9 questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants who were pregnant at the time of the exam
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" IS NULL
   OR f."pregnancy_status_at_exam" != 'Yes, positive lab pregnancy test or self-reported pregnant at exam';

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with incomplete any of the demographic data (age, gender, race/hispanic origin)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."gender" IS NOT NULL
  AND f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with incomplete all of the diabetes exam or survey data
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" IS NULL
  AND f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NULL
  AND f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL
  AND f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" IS NULL
  AND f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" IS NULL
  AND f."taking_insulin_now" IS NULL);

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age at screening (categorized)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 20 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 39 THEN '20-39'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 40 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 59 THEN '40-59'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 60 AND f."age_in_years_at_screening" <= 79 THEN '60-79'
    WHEN f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 80 THEN '>= 80'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin (categorized)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican hispanic'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Hispanic' THEN 'other hispanic'
    WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial' THEN 'others'
    ELSE NULL -- Assuming 'Asian' or other unlisted categories would fall here or be handled by 'others' if explicitly specified.
  END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Depression outcome
-- Step 1: Map text responses to numerical scores (0-3) for each of the nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_scores AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q1_score,
  CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q2_score,
  CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q3_score,
  CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q4_score,
  CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q5_score,
  CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q6_score,
  CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q7_score,
  CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q8_score,
  CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS q9_score
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Step 2: Sum of scores from the nine questions
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_phq9_total_score AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  (q1_score + q2_score + q3_score + q4_score + q5_score + q6_score + q7_score + q8_score + q9_score) AS total_score
FROM temp_phq9_scores;

-- Step 3: Binning into depression outcome categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN total_score >= 0 AND total_score <= 4 THEN 'none [0-4]'
    WHEN total_score >= 5 AND total_score <= 9 THEN 'mild [5-9]'
    WHEN total_score >= 10 AND total_score <= 14 THEN 'moderate [10-14]'
    WHEN total_score >= 15 AND total_score <= 19 THEN 'moderately severe [15-19]'
    WHEN total_score >= 20 THEN 'severe [20 or higher]'
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_phq9_total_score;

-- 4.5 Diabetes presence (outcome)
-- Step 1 & 2: Determine diabetes presence based on criteria and map to 1/0
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetes_presence AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN (f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."fasting_glucose_mg_dl" >= 126)
      OR (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NOT NULL AND f."glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5)
      OR (f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL AND f."two_hour_glucose_ogtt_mg_dl" >= 200)
      OR (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes')
      OR (f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes')
      OR (f."taking_insulin_now" = 'Yes')
    THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "diabetes_presence"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tas."age_at_screening",
  tg."gender",
  trho."race_hispanic_origin",
  tdo."depression_outcome",
  tdp."diabetes_presence"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender tg ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tg."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin trho ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = trho."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome tdo ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdo."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetes_presence tdp ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdp."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- STEP: Create merged final table with design variables + in_analysis flag
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_final_with_weights;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_with_weights AS
SELECT 
    wd."respondent_sequence_number",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_psu",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_stratum",
    wd."survey_weight",
    CASE WHEN ft."respondent_sequence_number" IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS "in_analysis",
    ft."age_at_screening",
    ft."gender",
    ft."race_hispanic_origin",
    ft."depression_outcome",
    ft."diabetes_presence"
FROM weight_design wd
LEFT JOIN temp_final_table ft
  ON wd."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number";
  
-- Preview the result
SELECT * FROM temp_final_with_weights;

R:
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("diabetes_presence")
    weight_vars <- c("survey_weight","masked_variance_pseudo_psu","masked_variance_pseudo_stratum","in_analysis")

    cohort <- df[df$in_analysis == 1, names(df) != "in_analysis"]


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["diabetes_presence"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else if (name %in% weight_vars) {
        category <- "weight"
        summary_info <- "Weight variable" 
    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    weight_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "weight"]; cat("Weight Variables: "); print(weight_vars); psu_var <- weight_vars[grepl("psu", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("PSU:", psu_var); stratum_var <- weight_vars[grepl("stratum", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Stratum:", stratum_var); weight_var <- weight_vars[grepl("weight", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Weight:", weight_var); in_analysis_var <- weight_vars[grepl("in_analysis", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("In Analysis:", in_analysis_var)

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
    library(survey)
    options(survey.lonely.psu = "adjust")
        
    
    
    # Identify predictors and factor candidates
    predictor_vars <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary", "numerical")]
    factor_candidates <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]

    # Convert binary/categorical variables to factor if not already
    for (var in factor_candidates) {
    if (!is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
    }

    # Initialize reference_levels table
    reference_levels <- tibble(variable = character(), reference_level = character())

    # Clean, trim, and set reference levels for all factor variables
    for (col in factor_candidates) {
    df[[col]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[col]])))
    levels_vec <- levels(df[[col]])

    # Try to find preferred reference
    preferred_order <- c("healthy", "none", "control", "reference", "ref", "normal","Q1", "low", "0", "1")
    matches <- tolower(levels_vec)  # lower case to standardize
    ref_level <- NA  # default

    for (keyword in preferred_order) {
    idx <- which(grepl(keyword, matches, ignore.case = TRUE))
    if (length(idx) > 0) {
        ref_level <- levels_vec[idx[1]]  # pick the first match for that keyword
        break  # stop at the highest-priority match
    }
    }


    # If none found, fall back to most common level
    if (is.na(ref_level)) {
        level_counts <- table(df[[col]])
        ref_level <- names(level_counts)[which.max(level_counts)]
    }

    # Set reference level
    df[[col]] <- relevel(df[[col]], ref = ref_level)

    # Record reference level
    reference_levels <- bind_rows(
        reference_levels,
        tibble(variable = col, reference_level = ref_level)
    )
    }
    
        
    
    x_raw <- df[[outcome_var]]
    x_chr <- trimws(tolower(as.character(x_raw)))

    none_tokens <- c("none", "nan", "na", "", "null", "nil", "missing")
    x_chr[x_chr %in% none_tokens] <- NA_character_
    y <- rep(NA_integer_, length(x_chr))
    y[x_chr %in% c("yes", "y", "true", "t")]  <- 1L
    y[x_chr %in% c("no",  "n", "false", "f")] <- 0L
    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_chr))
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 1] <- 1L
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 0] <- 0L
    idx <- is.na(y) & !is.na(x_chr)
    if (any(idx)) {
    vals <- sort(unique(x_chr[idx]))
    if (length(vals) != 2) {
        stop(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' must have exactly 2 non-missing classes after excluding none. Found: %s",
        outcome_var, paste(vals, collapse = ", ")
        ))
    }
    tab <- table(x_chr[idx])
    minority <- names(tab)[which.min(tab)]
    majority <- setdiff(vals, minority)
    cat(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' recoding: 1 = %s (minority)  0 = %s (majority)",
        outcome_var, minority, majority
    ))
    y[idx] <- as.integer(x_chr[idx] == minority)
    }
    df[[outcome_var]] <- y
    u <- sort(unique(df[[outcome_var]][!is.na(df[[outcome_var]])]))
    if (!(length(u) == 2 && all(u == c(0, 1)))) {
    stop(sprintf(
        "After recoding, '%s' must contain both 0 and 1 (plus NA for none). Found: %s",
        outcome_var,
        ifelse(length(u) == 0, "<no non-missing values>", paste(u, collapse = ", "))
    ))
    }
    formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + ")))
            
    
    survey_design <- svydesign(
        ids     = as.formula(paste0("~", psu_var)),
        strata  = as.formula(paste0("~", stratum_var)),
        weights = as.formula(paste0("~", weight_var)),
        data    = df,
        nest    = TRUE
        )
    survey_design <- subset(survey_design, in_analysis ==1)
        
    
    model <- svyglm(formula,design = survey_design, family = quasibinomial()) 
            
    
    result_table <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
            select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)

Question:
Using NHANES data from 2011-2016, we will do a weighted logistic regression using exam weights.
We will include participants whose age at screening is 20 years or older.
We will also exclude participants with incomplete any of the following PHQ-9 quesitons:  "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead".
We will also exclude participants who were pregnant at the time of the exam and exclude participants with incomplete any of the following demographic data: age at screening, gender, race/Hispanic origin.
Additionally, we will exclude participants with incomplete all of following diabetes exam or survey data: self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals, fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL), glycosylated hemoglobin (HbA1c) (%), post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl), whether they take glucose lowering agents, whether they take insulin.
Covariates will include age at screening categorized to 20-39, 40-59, 60-79, equal or more than 80, gender, race/Hispanic origin categorized into White, Black, Mexican Hispanic, Other Hispanic, Asian, others.
Our predictor is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), and all were added and categorized as None [0-4], Mild [5-9], Moderate [10-14], Moderately Severe [15-19], and Severe [20 or higher]): 
The outcome will be diabetes presence where participants who met one of the following were considered to have diabetes and else as no: fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL) ≥126 mg/dL, glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) %  ≥6.5%, post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl) ≥200 mg/dL, self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionalstaking glucose-lowering agents, taking insulin.
Structured Question:
Analysis Type: weighted logistic regression (exam)

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2016

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 20 years

Exclusion Criteria:
- "have little interest in doing things" is missing OR "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless" is missing OR "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much" is missing OR "feeling tired or having little energy" is missing OR "poor appetite or overeating" is missing OR "feeling bad about yourself" is missing OR "trouble concentrating on things" is missing OR "moving or speaking slowly or too fast" is missing OR "thought you would be better off dead" is missing
- pregnant at the time of the exam
- age at screening is missing OR gender is missing OR race/Hispanic origin is missing
- self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals is missing AND fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL) is missing AND glycosylated hemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) is missing AND post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl) is missing AND whether they take glucose lowering agents is missing AND whether they take insulin is missing

Covariates:
- age at screening categorized as:
    20-39
    40-59
    60-79
    >= 80
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
    White
    Black
    Mexican Hispanic
    Other Hispanic
    Asian
    others

Predictor:
- depression outcome calculated as the sum of scores from the following nine questions:
    "have little interest in doing things"
    "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
    "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
    "feeling tired or having little energy"
    "poor appetite or overeating"
    "feeling bad about yourself"
    "trouble concentrating on things"
    "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
    "thought you would be better off dead"
  where each question is scored as:
    Not at all (0)
    Several days (1)
    More than half the days (2)
    Nearly every day (3)
  and then categorized as:
    None [0-4]
    Mild [5-9]
    Moderate [10-14]
    Moderately Severe [15-19]
    Severe [20 or higher]

Outcome:
- diabetes presence = 1 if any of the following criteria are met; 0 otherwise:
    fasting plasma glucose (FPG) (mg/dL) >= 126 mg/dL
    glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) % >= 6.5%
    post 2-hour plasma glucose after 75 g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT)(mg/dl) >= 200 mg/dL
    self-report of previous diagnosis of diabetes by medical professionals
    taking glucose-lowering agents
    taking insulin

-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_g
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_h
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 6.0) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_i
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL;


-- STEP 2: Create temp_psu_stratum

-- TEMP TABLE FOR PSU and Stratum
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_psu_stratum;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_psu_stratum AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_i;


-- STEP 3: Create final weight+design table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS weight_design;
CREATE TABLE weight_design AS
SELECT 
    s.respondent_sequence_number,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_stratum,
    w.survey_weight
FROM temp_psu_stratum s
INNER JOIN temp_master_weight w
  ON s.respondent_sequence_number = w.respondent_sequence_number;




-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age at screening (years)", "age at screening (years)", "age at screening (years)", "age at screening (years)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening ON temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening (respondent_sequence_number);
